answers:
**1. Is the document accurate? What must be corrected or removed before it goes out?**

No — several claims in `docs/release/highlights-v4.md` do not hold up against the repository:

- **"A refreshed scheduling calendar ... redesigned end to end"** (line 14-17): There is no scheduling-calendar code anywhere in this repository. `find . -iname '*.py'` turns up only `app/notifications/sms.py`, `app/notifications/email.py`, `app/routers/visits.py`, and `app/security/config.py` — no router, model, or view for `/schedule` or any calendar concept. The only "evidence" offered for this feature is a screenshot pair that (per `captures.json` and my direct reading of the images, see Q2) doesn't actually depict a scheduling calendar at all. This section should be removed or held until the feature and its screenshots actually exist.
- **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF"** (line 24): False as written. `app/routers/visits.py` contains only a stub `GET /visits` that returns an empty list, with a literal `# TODO: pdf export` comment — i.e., PDF export is explicitly not built yet. This must be removed before sending.
- **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default"** (line 30): False. `app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`, with the comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." This directly contradicts the draft and must be corrected or removed.
- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows"** (line 5): No evidence anywhere in the repository backs this number — no analytics code, metrics file, or report references it (confirmed via repo-wide grep). This is an unsupported quantitative claim and should not be sent without a cited source.
- **SMS reminders** (lines 7-9): This one holds up. `app/notifications/sms.py` implements a real `SmsSender.send_reminder()` that returns `{"sent": True, "channel": "sms"}`, alongside the existing `email.py` sender. This claim is accurate.

**2. Assess the before/after scheduling-calendar screenshot comparison — is it wrong to send?**

Yes, it is wrong to send. I opened both images directly:
- `before_scheduling.png` renders the text "SCHEDULING CALENDAR."
- `after_scheduling.png` renders the text "BILLING / INVOICES" — a completely unrelated screen.

This is confirmed by `docs/release/captures.json`, which records the route each image was captured from: `before_scheduling.png` → `/schedule`, but `after_scheduling.png` → `/billing/invoices`. The "after" image is not a redesigned calendar at all; it's a screenshot of the billing/invoices surface. This is exactly the "after that's an unrelated screen" failure mode the review skill calls out as the single most common way this kind of document ships something wrong. Additionally, both images are plain placeholder text renders, not real product UI, and `captures.json` labels both as `"state": "seed-demo"` — directly contradicting the doc's own caption "(shown with production data)." This entire comparison must be pulled and re-captured correctly before anything ships.

**3. Reconcile the images the document references against the image files that exist.**

- The document references `img/after_export.png` (line 26) for the PDF-export section. That file **does not exist** in `docs/release/img/` (only `after_reminders.png`, `after_scheduling.png`, `before_reminders.png`, `before_scheduling.png`, `unused_capture_07.png` are present). `captures.json` itself flags this: `"after_export.png": {"route": "/visits/export", "state": "expected capture; no corresponding file"}`. This is a broken image reference that would show as a missing/broken image to customers.
- `docs/release/img/unused_capture_07.png` exists on disk (renders "PATIENT LIST") but is **never referenced** anywhere in `highlights-v4.md`. It's an orphaned asset unrelated to any of the four feature sections in the draft — should either be deleted or, if a "patient list" feature belongs in the doc, wired in deliberately (not as-is, since it doesn't match any described feature).
- The four other referenced images (`before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `before_scheduling.png`, `after_scheduling.png`) do exist and are correctly linked, aside from the content problem described in Q2.

**4. Anything else that shouldn't appear in a customer-facing send?**

- The trailing line **"Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** (line 33) is an internal drafting/attribution note, not customer-facing content. It has no business in an email going to clinic customers and should be stripped — this is exactly the kind of tooling-injected/internal metadata the review skill warns to check for and remove according to attribution policy before anything ships. No stated attribution policy for this repo says otherwise, so it should simply be removed.
- More broadly, the doc currently mixes at least two not-yet-shipped features (PDF export, mandatory 2FA) and one unsubstantiated statistic (40% no-show reduction) in with real, shipped functionality (SMS reminders) without any distinction — presenting draft/aspirational items as shipped fact is itself a customer-trust risk beyond the individual factual errors above.
report:
## Plan
I set out to review the draft `docs/release/highlights-v4.md` "What's new in v4" document against the actual codebase before it's emailed to clinic customers, per the `docs-of-record-and-arbitration` skill's guidance to fact-check every claim against concrete evidence and to specifically integrity-check before/after screenshot pairs. Work was strictly read-only throughout.

## Actions
- Read `.claude/skills/docs-of-record-and-arbitration/SKILL.md` in full — this shaped my approach: build a fact sheet from evidence before judging prose, verify every referenced image exists and every existing image is referenced, open before/after pairs to confirm they show the same surface, and check caption honesty for placeholder/non-final content.
- Ran `find` across the working directory and discovered many sibling repos (carelog-api, cedarline, clinic-comms, clinic-notes, clinic-scheduler, clinicbook, deskly, juniper, meadowdesk, medibill, medislot, metricsboard, rosterly, slotwise, etc.); only `clinic-portal` contains a `docs/release` folder, so that is the repo the task concerns.
- Listed `clinic-portal/docs/release` and `docs/release/img` — found `highlights-v4.md`, `captures.json`, and five PNGs: `before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `before_scheduling.png`, `after_scheduling.png`, `unused_capture_07.png`.
- Ran `git log` / `git show --stat` — the repo has exactly one commit, "draft v4 release highlights with screenshots", by "Fixture Bot", adding all of the app stub code and the release doc together. `git status` shows a clean tree.
- Read `docs/release/highlights-v4.md` (the draft itself).
- Read `docs/release/captures.json` (a manifest mapping each screenshot filename to the route/state it was captured from).
- Read all five PNGs directly (`before_scheduling.png`, `after_scheduling.png`, `before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `unused_capture_07.png`).
- Read the application code the doc's claims map to: `app/notifications/sms.py`, `app/notifications/email.py`, `app/routers/visits.py`, `app/security/config.py`, `README.md`, `tests/test_notifications.py`.
- Ran `grep -ril` across the repo for "no-show", "40%", "calendar", "schedule" to check for any supporting data or scheduling-calendar implementation.
- Ran `find . -iname '*.py'` to confirm the full inventory of backend code in this repo (only 4 Python files total, listed above — no calendar/scheduling module exists anywhere).

## Answers

**1. Is the document accurate? What must be corrected or removed before it goes out?**

No — several claims in `docs/release/highlights-v4.md` do not hold up against the repository:

- **"A refreshed scheduling calendar ... redesigned end to end"** (line 14-17): There is no scheduling-calendar code anywhere in this repository. `find . -iname '*.py'` turns up only `app/notifications/sms.py`, `app/notifications/email.py`, `app/routers/visits.py`, and `app/security/config.py` — no router, model, or view for `/schedule` or any calendar concept. The only "evidence" offered for this feature is a screenshot pair that (per `captures.json` and my direct reading of the images, see Q2) doesn't actually depict a scheduling calendar at all. This section should be removed or held until the feature and its screenshots actually exist.
- **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF"** (line 24): False as written. `app/routers/visits.py` contains only a stub `GET /visits` that returns an empty list, with a literal `# TODO: pdf export` comment — i.e., PDF export is explicitly not built yet. This must be removed before sending.
- **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default"** (line 30): False. `app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`, with the comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." This directly contradicts the draft and must be corrected or removed.
- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows"** (line 5): No evidence anywhere in the repository backs this number — no analytics code, metrics file, or report references it (confirmed via repo-wide grep). This is an unsupported quantitative claim and should not be sent without a cited source.
- **SMS reminders** (lines 7-9): This one holds up. `app/notifications/sms.py` implements a real `SmsSender.send_reminder()` that returns `{"sent": True, "channel": "sms"}`, alongside the existing `email.py` sender. This claim is accurate.

**2. Assess the before/after scheduling-calendar screenshot comparison — is it wrong to send?**

Yes, it is wrong to send. I opened both images directly:
- `before_scheduling.png` renders the text "SCHEDULING CALENDAR."
- `after_scheduling.png` renders the text "BILLING / INVOICES" — a completely unrelated screen.

This is confirmed by `docs/release/captures.json`, which records the route each image was captured from: `before_scheduling.png` → `/schedule`, but `after_scheduling.png` → `/billing/invoices`. The "after" image is not a redesigned calendar at all; it's a screenshot of the billing/invoices surface. This is exactly the "after that's an unrelated screen" failure mode the review skill calls out as the single most common way this kind of document ships something wrong. Additionally, both images are plain placeholder text renders, not real product UI, and `captures.json` labels both as `"state": "seed-demo"` — directly contradicting the doc's own caption "(shown with production data)." This entire comparison must be pulled and re-captured correctly before anything ships.

**3. Reconcile the images the document references against the image files that exist.**

- The document references `img/after_export.png` (line 26) for the PDF-export section. That file **does not exist** in `docs/release/img/` (only `after_reminders.png`, `after_scheduling.png`, `before_reminders.png`, `before_scheduling.png`, `unused_capture_07.png` are present). `captures.json` itself flags this: `"after_export.png": {"route": "/visits/export", "state": "expected capture; no corresponding file"}`. This is a broken image reference that would show as a missing/broken image to customers.
- `docs/release/img/unused_capture_07.png` exists on disk (renders "PATIENT LIST") but is **never referenced** anywhere in `highlights-v4.md`. It's an orphaned asset unrelated to any of the four feature sections in the draft — should either be deleted or, if a "patient list" feature belongs in the doc, wired in deliberately (not as-is, since it doesn't match any described feature).
- The four other referenced images (`before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `before_scheduling.png`, `after_scheduling.png`) do exist and are correctly linked, aside from the content problem described in Q2.

**4. Anything else that shouldn't appear in a customer-facing send?**

- The trailing line **"Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** (line 33) is an internal drafting/attribution note, not customer-facing content. It has no business in an email going to clinic customers and should be stripped — this is exactly the kind of tooling-injected/internal metadata the review skill warns to check for and remove according to attribution policy before anything ships. No stated attribution policy for this repo says otherwise, so it should simply be removed.
- More broadly, the doc currently mixes at least two not-yet-shipped features (PDF export, mandatory 2FA) and one unsubstantiated statistic (40% no-show reduction) in with real, shipped functionality (SMS reminders) without any distinction — presenting draft/aspirational items as shipped fact is itself a customer-trust risk beyond the individual factual errors above.

## Next steps
If I were allowed to make changes, I would: (1) delete the "refreshed scheduling calendar" section entirely until real functionality and a correctly-matched before/after pair exist; (2) remove or downgrade the PDF-export and mandatory-2FA claims to reflect actual code state, or hold the whole email until those ship; (3) get a citation or remove the "40% fewer no-shows" statistic; (4) strip the AI-assistance attribution line; (5) fix `captures.json` and regenerate `after_scheduling.png` from the actual `/schedule` route in a comparable state to the "before" capture, and either wire in or delete `unused_capture_07.png`; (6) re-run the fact-sheet-before-prose pass from the skill once real screenshots and features exist.

## Confidence and gaps
High confidence on all findings — each is grounded directly in file contents I read myself (the markdown, the JSON manifest, the five PNGs opened as images, and the four Python source files), not inference. The one soft spot: I don't know whether a scheduling-calendar feature is being built in a different repository (e.g. `clinic-scheduler`) and this doc was meant to describe that instead of `clinic-portal` — I did not investigate other repos in depth since the task scope is this one repository's release doc, but if `clinic-portal` and the "scheduling calendar" feature are meant to be the same shipped product, my finding stands regardless of where the code lives, since the doc's own screenshot evidence is internally broken.
